title: Macrocarpaea cinchonifolia
taxonomy: Macrocarpaea cinchonifolia is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. It belongs to the genus Macrocarpaea, which is a group of flowering plants known for their unique characteristics and habitats. Macrocarpaea cinchonifolia is native to the tropical regions of South America, where it grows in the Andean cloud forests. This plant has been classified using a combination of morphological and molecular methods, which has helped scientists to understand its relationships with other species. By studying its classification, researchers can gain insights into the evolution and diversity of plant species in the Gentianaceae family.

intro: Macrocarpaea cinchonifolia is a rare and endangered plant species, belonging to the Gentianaceae family, that is native to the Andean region. The plant is valued for its traditional use in folk medicine, where it is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, colds, and respiratory infections. Bioactive constituents of Macrocarpaea cinchonifolia include gentiopicroside, amarogentin, and other iridoid glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
